company.name: Dyne Therapeutics
company.one_liner: FORCE platform delivering oligonucleotide therapies to muscle — lead programs in DM1 and Duchenne
company.company_description: Dyne Therapeutics is developing muscle-targeted RNA therapeutics using its FORCE (Fab-Oligonucleotide Conjugate for Effective delivery) platform. The platform conjugates antibody fragments to oligonucleotides for enhanced muscle delivery. Lead programs target myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) and Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD).

investment_thesis_summary.core_thesis: Dyne's FORCE platform solves the oligonucleotide muscle delivery problem that has limited prior RNA therapies. If DYNE-101 confirms Phase 1/2 splicing correction and functional improvement in DM1, this platform could dominate muscle genetic diseases — a space with minimal competition and high unmet need.
key_value_drivers: DYNE-101: FORCE-conjugated antisense for DM1 — Phase 1/2 showing meaningful splicing correction
DYNE-251: FORCE-conjugated exon-skipping for DMD — competing with Sarepta's gene therapy approach
FORCE platform: antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates achieve 10-30x greater muscle exposure than naked oligos
Large rare disease markets: DM1 (~80K US patients), DMD (~15K US) with few effective treatments

bull_case: DYNE-101: FORCE-conjugated antisense for DM1 — Phase 1/2 showing meaningful splicing correction
DYNE-251: FORCE-conjugated exon-skipping for DMD — competing with Sarepta's gene therapy approach
FORCE platform: antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates achieve 10-30x greater muscle exposure than naked oligos
bear_case: Awaiting detailed extraction from IR presentations
